headline: GOLD FREEZES ABOVE $4,700 AS IRAN REFUSES TO REOPEN HORMUZ; SILVER SLIPS TO $76
snippet: Gold held above $4,737/oz as Trump extended the US-Iran ceasefire but Tehran refused to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, keeping inflation fears elevated and weighing on silver near $76/oz for the week.

headline: CONTANGO LAYS OUT $63M ALASKA-BC EXPLORATION BLITZ; $25M TO DRILL 40,000 METRES AT KITSAULT
snippet: Contango Silver & Gold outlined a $63M 2026 exploration program anchored by a $25M push at Kitsault Valley in Northern BC, including 40,000 metres of infill drilling across its consolidated Alaska and BC gold and critical metals portfolio.

headline: GOLDSEEK COT REPORT: GOLD, SILVER AND USDX POSITIONING — WEEK OF APRIL 24
snippet: Weekly Commitment of Traders breakdown covering large speculator and commercial hedger positioning in gold and silver futures alongside the US dollar index, as of the April 24 reporting period.

headline: KAMLOOPS JUNIOR GOLD: 2026 DRILL UPDATE AND FRESH ANALYST PRICE TARGETS ON THE CHARTS
snippet: A junior BC gold explorer releases 2026 drilling results alongside revised analyst price targets, benefiting from gold above $4,700 and the widening margin gap between metal prices and all-in sustaining costs.

headline: COPPER BACK ABOVE $6/LB AS CHINA RESTOCKS BEFORE LABOR DAY; MARCH SMELTER OUTPUT SETS ALL-TIME RECORD
snippet: Copper futures rebounded above $6/lb on April 23 as Chinese fabricators restocked ahead of the May 1-5 Labor Day holiday; China's March refined copper output set a record 1.33 million tonnes — the highest in data going back to 1990.

headline: UR-ENERGY FIRES UP SHIRLEY BASIN: FIRST NEW U.S. URANIUM ISR MINE IN OVER A DECADE
snippet: Ur-Energy (NYSE-A: URG) commenced uranium recovery at its Shirley Basin ISR project in Wyoming on April 23 — the first new in-situ recovery operation to start up in the U.S. in over a decade — with a licensed capacity of 2M lbs U₃O₈/year.

headline: GOLD, SILVER AND COPPER: ALL THREE METALS NOW IN STRUCTURAL BULL PHASE AS SUPPLY GAPS WIDEN
snippet: Analysis finds all three metals underpinned by structural supply shortfalls: central bank gold buying has tripled to 1,000+ tonnes per year; silver enters its sixth consecutive deficit; copper supply is forecast to fall 10 million tonnes short of demand by 2040.

headline: USA RARE EARTH BETS $2.8B ON BRAZIL'S SERRA VERDE TO BREAK CHINA'S MAGNETIC RARE EARTH GRIP
snippet: USA Rare Earth (USAR) agreed to acquire Serra Verde for $300M cash plus $126.9M in stock — the only producer outside Asia capable of supplying all four magnetic rare earths at scale; USAR surged 13% on the news and is up 93% in 2026.

headline: ATEX RESOURCES RESUMES PHASED DRILLING AT VALERIANO COPPER-GOLD PROJECT IN CHILE
snippet: ATEX Resources (TSXV: ATX) began a phased return to work at its 2-billion-tonne Valeriano copper-gold porphyry in Chile's Atacama Region on April 20, after a brief regulatory suspension triggered by a contractor incident on April 15.

headline: SILVER FACES SIXTH STRAIGHT SUPPLY DEFICIT IN 2026; SILVER INSTITUTE PROJECTS 67M-OZ SHORTFALL
snippet: The Silver Institute projects a 67 million ounce silver supply deficit for 2026 — the sixth consecutive annual shortfall — as physical investment demand surges 20% to 227Moz while mine supply grows just 1%, forcing continued drawdowns from above-ground inventories.
